title: Hey everyone , I m looking for tools /websites that simply journal my trades on automation without me having to write about them manually , like they simply connect to my broker and record these trades
author: PuzzleheadedDoor6336
selftext: I have recently started journalling my trades, its been around a month , i m using google sheets however tbh having to write about the trades myself is somewhat of a hassle and i m not really a big fan of that so i am seeking journal tools / websites that journal my trades purely via automation by connecting to my broker and help me detect any common patterns that make me repeat the mistakes.

comments.author: kwame1776
comments.body: I’m building an app like that. It’s a trading discipline system which helps with planning trades but it will also auto journal them as well. It’s called Gatekeeper.

comments.author: Past-Relation-5325
comments.body: Alright there r some tools which might help you with this  - Tradexis, Tradezella , Trading edge , just see which one's cheaper i reckon the 1st is free ,  but I m not so sure so just check it out for urself,

replies.author: PuzzleheadedDoor6336
replies.body: How long have u been journalling ur trades if i may ask and does it even help in the long run

comments.author: Nirbhay_106
comments.body: Yeah honestly, manuallimg journaling on excel/gooogler sheets is somewhat time consuming , may go for tradervue , trader sync or tradexis .ai  , as these tools integrate directly to the free and r prob cheaper in comparison to other similar platforms. Happy trading , Cheers!

replies.author: PuzzleheadedDoor6336
replies.body: Okay , thanks man and yeah jotting down those points manually is really difficult, thats the main reason why i m switching to automated ones

comments.author: Opening_Kitchen_5349
comments.body: You might want to check out SuperTrader trading journal. It connects directly to supported brokers and automatically imports and journals your trades, so you don't have to manually log everything in spreadsheets. The platform also tracks performance metrics and helps identify recurring patterns in your trading behavior, which can be useful for spotting mistakes you're making repeatedly.

replies.author: PuzzleheadedDoor6336
replies.body: Alright but is it like free

replies.author: Opening_Kitchen_5349
replies.body: Not completely free. It comes with a 7-day free trial, and after that the monthly plan is only $9.99$ Compared to most other trading journals, it's much more affordable while still offering automated trade imports and detailed analytics.

comments.author: Ojasisamyth
comments.body: The market is full of options for these however since u mentioned u have just started journalling , the ones with easy UI and automated journalling that connects directly with the broker are Tradexis , Stonk jounral and Tradezella , these options have a simpler UI and woukd definitely help u if u r just starting out

replies.author: PuzzleheadedDoor6336
replies.body: Alright man , is it free tho??

replies.author: Ojasisamyth
replies.body: Hmm i reckon the first 2 are free or cheaper but i m not so sure so just check it out urself , Cheers!

comments.author: Musicklover
comments.body: I believe the gold is where you don’t think it is. It’s in that tedious manual journaling. That’s how you learn. I have an automatic journaling through my Ninjatrader platform, and it’s so easy to save the trades and it will calculate some kind of metrics for you like MAE, MFE, etc. , I can save these and never see again.However, I believe the benefit is the act of going over each of your trades, making a dissertation out of these,.. a thesis..like a pHD. Defend what’s showing in the journal, at the end see whether you’ve defended enough to justify your keeping trading for the next day. If not, get yourself with an accountabilty partner because you can’t police yourself. I will use AI because I don’t have any. I’ll set codex to email my wife my secret stash if I don’t follow my rules.

comments.author: Annual-Register-3683
comments.body:
bro if you've been journaling for a month already, you're ahead of a lot of traders. The problem is that manually entering every trade can become a chore, and once it feels like work, it's easy to stop doing it consistently.

There are a few journaling platforms that connect directly to brokers and import trades automatically. What I like is that they can highlight patterns you might not notice yourself, such as overtrading, poor risk management, or certain times of day when you perform better or worse. I still add a few notes here and there, but automating the tracking saves a lot of time. I also run my platform through tradingfx vps so everything stays connected and records properly while I'm focused on trading.

comments.author: Maximum_Goose5200
comments.body:
As a forex trader, I built a pretty simple auto-journaling system with Python and Obsidian.

First, I wrote a basic MT5 EA that dumps my trade history along with OHLC data from recent candles into an Excel file.

Then a Python script reads that file, recreates the chart with the candles and my trades on it, and exports it as an image.

The script also sends the trade details and chart image into an Obsidian note, so every trade gets documented automatically.

At the end of the day, another Python script sends all my trading data to an LLM. Based on my strategy, starting balance, ending balance, win rate, losses, etc., it gives me feedback and highlights things I should pay attention to.

I don't use the LLM to generate signals. I treat it more like a trading coach that reviews my performance and reminds me when I'm drifting away from my own rules.

replies.author: Maximum_Goose5200
replies.body: plus cost zero except the cost of API for LLM (chatgpt)

comments.author: eggrally
comments.body: Simple, just tell ai to trade your trades,  have the bot connect to the broker via api
